'''Simon Booth''' (born 9 December 1971) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[Super League]], [[Hull FC]] and [[Hull KR]] as a {{rlp|sr}}.

==Playing career==
Booth joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] from [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in 1994.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Club-by-club guide to the new season |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-clubbyclub-guide-to-the-new-season-1377408.html |website=The Independent |access-date=16 February 2024 |date=18 August 1994}}</ref> He played for St Helens at {{rlp|sr}} in the [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final, scoring a try in the second half and helping his team to a 40–32 victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012}}</ref>

Booth played at {{rlp|sr}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015}}</ref>
